body:
## Sync service

Stand up the `services/sync` service — keeps a user's notes consistent across devices.

### Scope
- [ ] WebSocket endpoint clients connect to (authenticated via JWT)
- [ ] Broadcast `SyncEvent`s to a user's other connected devices
- [ ] Per-user connection registry / presence
- [ ] Accept change events from notes-api
- [ ] Sketch offline reconciliation using the note `version` field (last-write-wins to start)

### Notes
- Use the shared `SyncEvent` type and `SYNC_PROTOCOL_VERSION`.
- Depends on auth (token validation) and notes-api (change source).
- Start with last-write-wins; revisit CRDT/OT later if needed.

Part of the project kickoff. Tracked via branch `feat/sync-service` and its PR.
title: Sync service: real-time note sync across devices

body:
## Notes CRUD API

Stand up the `services/notes-api` service — owns note persistence.

### Scope
- [ ] `POST   /notes`        create a note
- [ ] `GET    /notes`        list the authenticated user's notes
- [ ] `GET    /notes/:id`    fetch one note
- [ ] `PATCH  /notes/:id`    update title/body (bumps `version`)
- [ ] `DELETE /notes/:id`    delete a note
- [ ] JWT auth middleware (validates tokens issued by the auth service)
- [ ] Ownership checks (a user can only touch their own notes)

### Notes
- Use the shared `Note` type. Bump `version` and `updatedAt` on every write.
- Emit a `SyncEvent` on each mutation so the sync service can pick it up (stub/log for now).
- Depends on the auth service for token validation.

Part of the project kickoff. Tracked via branch `feat/notes-api` and its PR.
title: Notes API: CRUD endpoints for notes

body:
## Auth service

Stand up the `services/auth` service — the source of truth for user identity.

### Scope
- [ ] User registration (email + password, hashed with bcrypt)
- [ ] Login endpoint returning a signed JWT
- [ ] JWT issuance using the shared `AuthTokenClaims` shape
- [ ] `/verify` (or middleware export) so other services can validate tokens
- [ ] Health check endpoint

### Notes
- Use `@notabene/shared` for the `User` and `AuthTokenClaims` types.
- In-memory store is fine for the initial cut; swap for a real DB later.
- This unblocks notes-api (which validates these JWTs) and sync.

Part of the project kickoff. Tracked via branch `feat/auth-service` and its PR.
title: Auth service: user registration, login, and JWT issuance
